bullets.text: January PPI rose 0.5%; core wholesale prices +0.8% (hotter than expected). Services drove gains while goods fell, complicating disinflation narrative.
Despite hot PPI, 10-year Treasury yield fell below 4% as investors priced higher stagflation risk and growth concerns.
Trump ordered U.S. agencies to stop using Anthropic; Pentagon moving to label it a supply-chain risk, sharpening AI procurement and safety-policy conflict.
Silicon Valley backlash grows: employees at Google and OpenAI publicly supported Anthropic, highlighting widening industry-government rift over military AI rules.
OpenAI raised $110B at ~$730B pre-money; ChatGPT reported 900M weekly active users—signaling extreme capital concentration and platform-scale AI adoption.
AI competition and monetization strains: Nvidia weakness on competition fears; CoreWeave shares plunged on debt/spending concerns; Dell surged on AI-server demand despite memory costs.
Block cut about half its workforce, fueling debate that AI is already replacing jobs and accelerating tech-sector labor restructuring.
U.S.-Iran nuclear talks ended without a deal; Trump signaled possible force. Oil markets priced higher strike odds, raising geopolitical supply-risk premium.
USS Gerald R. Ford heading to the Middle East as Iran tensions rise—visible escalation of U.S. military posture and deterrence signaling.
Russia and Ukraine agreed to a localized truce to repair Europe’s largest nuclear plant at Zaporizhzhia, reducing immediate nuclear-accident risk.
Medicare trust fund projected to run out 12 years earlier than expected, intensifying U.S. fiscal sustainability pressures and future benefit/tax policy tradeoffs.
NASA overhauled Artemis amid safety concerns and delays, effectively pushing back a 2027 moon landing and reshaping near-term U.S. lunar exploration timelines.
Lab advance: Qt45 polymerase ribozyme can synthesize itself, strengthening hypotheses about plausible RNA-based early life and accelerating experimental origins-of-life research.
PFAS exposure, common in Americans, linked to faster biological aging—raising public-health risk estimates and likely increasing pressure for tighter regulation and remediation.
